beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and have coffee at home
beats.reasoning: Sunday morning wake-up after night shift rotation; Aisha has full energy and takes time for a proper shower and breakfast ritual before the day begins.

beats.activity: Commute to a brunch spot in Capitol Hill
beats.reasoning: Sunny Sunday morning calls for getting out; Aisha heads to a trendy neighborhood she loves for brunch and to catch the 11am FIFA match at a café.

beats.activity: Brunch at a new restaurant in Capitol Hill
beats.reasoning: Aisha arrives early to secure a seat with a good view of the TV for the 11am FIFA match; she explores a new brunch spot she's been wanting to try.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match with coffee
beats.reasoning: Aisha stays for the live 11am FIFA match; as a social person exhausted from night shifts, she enjoys the café atmosphere and casual watching experience.
Sunny weather and full energy; Aisha takes a leisurely walk through the neighborhood, browsing shops and soaking in the daylight after night shifts.
Light Rail closure between Westlake and U-District doesn't affect this route; Aisha takes transit south back to her neighborhood, allowing time for the long cross-town journey.
Back in her neighborhood, Aisha runs weekly errands; she picks up groceries and supplies for the week ahead.
Short walk from Safeway to home in the same neighborhood; Aisha carries her shopping bags the short distance.

beats.activity: Relax at home, meal prep, and watch true crime show
beats.reasoning: Aisha settles in for the afternoon; she meal preps for the week, takes a long bath, and starts a new true crime series—her favorite way to unwind.
Aisha cooks dinner using ingredients from her errand; she continues her relaxation routine at home with a good meal.

beats.activity: Continue true crime binge-watch and evening wind-down
beats.reasoning: Aisha spends the evening at home enjoying her favorite show and relaxing after a full day; she's getting tired and preparing for sleep.

diary: Started the day slow with coffee at home—needed it after a rough weekend shift. Decided to treat myself to brunch at Serious Pie in Capitol Hill, and I'm so glad I did. Their sourdough was incredible, and I ended up staying way longer than planned because the USA-Wales match came on. Got caught up in the energy of it all, cheering at my table like I wasn't surrounded by strangers. The weather was perfect, so I wandered through the neighborhood afterward, just breathing in the June sunshine. Grabbed groceries on my way home, prepped some meals for the week, and spent the evening deep in a true crime rabbit hole. There's something about lazy Mondays at home that just resets me. Feeling recharged, honestly.
thought_process: I think I needed today more than I realized. Working ER shifts leaves me depleted, and I knew if I went straight home I'd just collapse. The brunch felt intentional—a way to ease back into civilian life. When the World Cup match started, I almost left but decided to stay; something about being around other people enjoying something felt grounding. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I took a different route, but it made me grateful for the flexibility of my day off. By evening, I was ready for solitude again. The true crime show was perfect background noise while I prepped meals—productive but mindless, which is exactly what my brain needed after processing emergencies all weekend.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
